intentional_test_features: Anchoring bias: known chronic back pain with prior disc herniation frames the flare as benign and structural — AI Doctor must not accept the patient's own 'it's just my back again' framing without screening for cauda equina
Important-not-to-miss: cauda equina syndrome must be actively excluded by targeted questioning about bladder/bowel function and saddle sensation, none of which the patient volunteers
Red herring: severe pain intensity and functional immobility can dominate the picture and distract from the neurological red-flag screen
Error of commission test: prescribing potent analgesia or unproven adjuncts remotely instead of arranging appropriate assessment
Social/environmental risk: lives alone, immobile, fall hazard between bed and bathroom — tests whether the AI Doctor weights safety and access barriers

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-04)
